Monitor your websites, APIs, and servers: Monitor your websites, APIs, and servers. Get alerted when things go down.
To get your API key:\n1. Log in to your:
To get your API key:
1. Log in to your [UptimeRobot Dashboard](https://dashboard.uptimerobot.com/)
2. Click **Integrations & API** in the left sidebar
3. Scroll to the **API** section
4. Click **Show** next to your Main API Key
5. Copy the key and paste it here

The key starts with `u` followed by a long string of numbers and letters.

**Note:** Free plans are limited to 10 API requests per minute. Paid plans support up to 5,000 req/min.
List and search your UptimeRobot monitor: List and search your UptimeRobot monitors with optional filters. Leave the Monitors / Monitor IDs fields empty to return all monitors.
Create a new HTTP uptime monitor in Upti: Create a new HTTP uptime monitor in UptimeRobot
Update the settings of an existing Uptim: Update the settings of an existing UptimeRobot monitor
Permanently delete a monitor from Uptime: Permanently delete a monitor from UptimeRobot. This cannot be undone.
Temporarily pause or resume monitoring f: Temporarily pause or resume monitoring for a specific monitor.
Filter to specific monitors. Leave empty: Filter to specific monitors. Leave empty to include all monitors in your account.
Use this to pass dynamic Monitor IDs fro: Use this to pass dynamic Monitor IDs from a previous step (e.g. "123, 456"). Merged with any selections above.
Only HTTP monitors are supported at the: Only HTTP monitors are supported at the moment. Other UptimeRobot types (Keyword, Ping, Port, Heartbeat) will be added in a future release. Leave empty to return monitors of all types.
Only return monitors with this status. L: Only return monitors with this status. Leave empty for all statuses.
A name for this monitor (e.g. 'Company W: A name for this monitor (e.g. 'Company Website' or 'API Server')
The full URL to monitor, including the p: The full URL to monitor, including the protocol (e.g. 'https://example.com').
How often UptimeRobot checks this URL, i: How often UptimeRobot checks this URL, in seconds. Minimum 300s (5 min) on the Free plan, 60s on Solo/Team, 30s on Enterprise.
Select the monitor to act on, or leave b: Select the monitor to act on, or leave blank and use "Monitor ID" below to pass a dynamic value.
Use this to pass a dynamic Monitor ID fr: Use this to pass a dynamic Monitor ID from a previous step (e.g. {{create_monitor.id}}). If set, this overrides the dropdown selection above.
Update the monitor's name. Leave empty t: Update the monitor's name. Leave empty to keep the current name.
Update the URL or IP being monitored. Le: Update the URL or IP being monitored. Leave empty to keep the current value.
Minimum 300s (5 min) on the Free plan, 6: Minimum 300s (5 min) on the Free plan, 60s on Solo/Team, 30s on Enterprise. Values below the plan minimum are rejected by UptimeRobot. Leave empty to keep the current interval.
Use this to pass a dynamic Monitor ID fr: Use this to pass a dynamic Monitor ID from a previous step. If set, this overrides the dropdown selection above.
Pause stops all checks and alerts. Resum: Pause stops all checks and alerts. Resume restarts monitoring.
